hero.headline: A new home begins with knowing your way.
hero.subheadline: Wayfinder finds the benefits and paperwork you qualify for, explains everything in your language, and guides you step by step, before deadlines pass.

how.title: Three steps from arrival to a clear plan.

steps.title: Tell us your story
steps.body: Answer a few simple questions, one at a time, in your own language. Large text, plain words, no rush.

steps.title: We find what fits
steps.body: Wayfinder checks a curated database of U.S. benefit rules and shows exactly what you qualify for, with sources, never guesses.

steps.title: Act in time
steps.body: Get a plan ranked by deadline, with every form explained and real human help nearby when it matters.

features.title: Everything you need, in one calm place.

items.title: My Action Plan
items.body: Your benefits ranked by soonest deadline, each with a plain-language reason you qualify and a countdown so nothing slips by.

items.title: AI Form Assistant
items.body: Wayfinder explains each field and drafts answers in your language. It never touches sensitive numbers, and never submits anything for you.

items.title: Document Vault
items.body: Keep your passport, I-94, and EAD in one encrypted, owner-only place. Private, and never shared with enforcement.

items.title: Find Help
items.body: For anything legal, Wayfinder connects you to a licensed attorney or accredited representative near you. A person, not a guess.

languagesSection.title: In your words, from the very first screen.
languagesSection.body: Pick your language before you sign up, and Wayfinder speaks it everywhere, every step of the way.

trust.title: We never share your information with immigration enforcement.
trust.body: Your documents are encrypted and owner-only. Wayfinder never fills sensitive identifiers, never submits forms, and every legal decision is made by a real, licensed person, not by a machine.

finalCta.title: Your way forward starts today.
finalCta.body: Free, private, and in your language. Find what you qualify for in minutes.

footer.tagline: Not legal advice · Always confirmed by a person

lpr_from_humanitarian.label: Green card from refugee, asylee, or humanitarian status
lpr_from_humanitarian.note: This restores SNAP and Medicaid eligibility

other_none.label: Other, not sure, or none of these

language.question: What language do you prefer?
language.hint: We will show everything in this language.

scan.question: Have your papers? Take a photo to save time
scan.intro: Snap a photo of your I-94 (and your work permit, green card, or asylum letter if you have them). We'll read your status and dates so you don't have to type them.
scan.secureLine: Your documents are processed securely in the moment and are never saved on our servers or shared with immigration enforcement. We read only the few fields needed to check your benefits — never your Social Security number or A-Number.
scan.uploadAriaLabel: Upload a photo or PDF of your immigration documents
scan.chooseFile: Take a photo or choose a file
scan.fileTypes: JPEG, PNG, or PDF · up to 4 documents
scan.skip: I don't have my documents — answer questions instead
scan.errorReadFailed: We couldn't read your document. Please try again, or skip and answer the questions.
scan.errorUnreachable: We couldn't reach the document reader. Please try again, or skip and answer the questions.

confirm.question: Please check what we read from your documents
confirm.intro: Your benefit results depend on these values, so take a moment to confirm them. Tap any field to fix it.
confirm.lowConfidence: Some values were hard to read (marked Please check below). Please double-check those first.

fields.fullNameHint: For your records only — not used for eligibility.
fields.dateOfBirthHint: Sets your age for age-based programs.
fields.arrivalDate: Date you arrived in the U.S.

status.question: What is your immigration status now?
status.hint: Choose the one that best fits you. This shapes almost every benefit rule.

documents.question: A couple of quick document questions
documents.hint: Tap Yes or No. It is okay if you do not have some.
documents.i94Hint: Proof of entry. An I-94 coded §207 or 'Visa 93' proves refugee status.
documents.eadHint: Employment Authorization Document. EAD code A03 also proves refugee status.
documents.ssnLabel: Do you have a Social Security Number (SSN)?
documents.ssnHint: We only need to know if you have one — never the number itself.

orrLetter.question: Do you have an ORR certification or eligibility letter?
orrLetter.hint: Trafficking victims need this letter (not an I-94) to access ORR benefits like RCA and RMA.

eligibilityDate.question: When did your refugee help officially start?
eligibilityDate.hint: This is your ORR eligibility date — usually your arrival date or the date your status was granted, whichever is later.
eligibilityDate.deadlineBody: The clock for Refugee Cash Assistance (RCA) and medical assistance starts from this date — apply right away.

docHintByStatus.refugee_207: Look on your I-94 (class RE-1 through RE-5, §207, or Visa 93). The date of admission is your ORR eligibility date.
docHintByStatus.asylee_208: Look on your asylum approval notice or the immigration judge's order (class AS, §208, or Visa 92). The date asylum was granted is your ORR eligibility date.
docHintByStatus.siv: Look on your I-94 or SIV visa stamp in your passport. Your date of entry is your ORR eligibility date.
docHintByStatus.afghan_parolee: Look on your I-94 (class OAR, PAR, or DT). Your date of entry is your ORR eligibility date.
docHintByStatus.ukrainian_parolee: Look on your I-94 (class UHP). Your date of entry is your ORR eligibility date.
docHintByStatus.cuban_haitian_entrant: Look on your I-94 or parole document. Your date of entry or parole is your ORR eligibility date.
docHintByStatus.trafficking_victim: Look on your ORR certification or eligibility letter. The certification date is your ORR eligibility date.
docHintByStatus.amerasian: Look on your I-94 or immigrant visa. Your date of entry is your ORR eligibility date.

arrivalDate.question: When did you come to the United States?
arrivalDate.hint: Most employment and integration services run up to 5 years from this date.

statusGrantDate.question: When were you granted refugee or asylee status?
statusGrantDate.hint: SSI eligibility ends 7 years from this date. Leave blank if you are not sure.

location.question: Which state do you live in?
location.hint: Benefits and local help vary by state. City and ZIP are optional.

household.question: Tell us about your household
household.hint: These answers set your income limits. They are private.
household.ageFromDoc: Your age ({age}) is set from your document. You can change it on the confirmation screen.
household.sizeLabel: How many people live with you, counting you?
household.incomeLabel: Money your household gets each month, before taxes (US dollars). Enter 0 if none.

children.question: How many children live with you?
children.under19Label: How many children under 19 live with you?
children.under18Label: Of those, how many are under 18?
children.under5Label: Of those, how many are under 5?
children.subsetHint: This is a smaller part of the number above.

pregnantCash.question: Two quick questions about your home
pregnantCash.pregnantLabel: Is anyone in your home pregnant now?
pregnantCash.pregnantHint: WIC and some Medicaid programs prioritize pregnant women.
pregnantCash.cashLabel: Do you already get cash help (TANF, SSI, or general assistance)?
pregnantCash.cashHint: This affects eligibility for Refugee Cash Assistance and SNAP.

special.question: A few special situations
special.hint: Tap Yes or No for each.
special.unaccompaniedLabel: Are you in the U.S. without a parent or legal guardian?
special.unaccompaniedHint: For the Unaccompanied Refugee Minors (URM) program.
special.disabledLabel: Do you have a disability that limits your work?
special.blindLabel: Are you blind or have very limited vision?
special.workQuartersLabel: Have you or a spouse or parent worked in the U.S. about 10 years?
special.workQuartersHint: 40 work credits can bypass some 5-year bars for SSI and SNAP.

goals.question: What would you like help with?
goals.hint: This helps us find the right programs for you.
goals.employmentLabel: Are you working or looking for work or job training?
goals.employmentHint: Employment services, childcare support, Matching Grant.
goals.businessLabel: Do you want to start or grow your own business?
goals.businessHint: Microenterprise development and Individual Development Accounts (IDA).
goals.englishHint: Free English language training through Refugee Support Services.
goals.interpreterLabel: Do you need an interpreter or translation help? (optional)
goals.interpreterHint: Free interpretation services are available through refugee programs.

settingsForm.i94Label: Do you have an I-94 Arrival/Departure Record?
settingsForm.eadLabel: Do you have a Work Permit (EAD card)?
settingsForm.eligibilityDateLabel: When did your refugee help officially start? (ORR eligibility date)
settingsForm.arrivalDateLabel: When did you come to the United States?
settingsForm.statusGrantDateLabel: When were you granted refugee or asylee status? (optional)

errors.saveFailed: We couldn't save your answers: {message}. If this mentions a missing column, the database migration (supabase/migration_v2.sql) hasn't been applied yet.
errors.profileNotFound: We couldn't find your profile to save your answers. Please sign out and sign back in, then try again.
errors.profileNotFoundShort: We could not find your profile to save. Please sign out and sign back in, then try again.

processing.title: Finding your benefits...
processing.subtitle: We are reviewing your answers against official U.S. benefit programs. This usually takes about 30 seconds.
processing.readySubtitle: We found benefits you may qualify for.

actionPlan.title: Your Action Plan
actionPlan.subtitle: Sorted by most urgent deadline first.
actionPlan.attorney: This requires an attorney or accredited representative.
actionPlan.noResults: No eligible benefits found. Answer more questions or contact a local organization for help.

documents.title: My Documents
documents.subtitle: Your files are encrypted and private. We never share them with immigration enforcement.
documents.dragDrop: Drag and drop a file, or click to choose
documents.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this document?
documents.secureLine: Your documents are processed securely and never shared with immigration enforcement.

formHelper.title: Form Assistant
formHelper.subtitle: Get help understanding and filling out a form. We never fill in your SSN, A-number, bank details, or passport number — those stay private.
formHelper.pasteInstructions: Paste the form name or URL, or describe what you need help with:
formHelper.placeholder: Example: I-765 Employment Authorization, or "help me fill out a Medicaid application"
formHelper.sensitiveNote: Fields marked [YOU FILL IN] contain sensitive information — complete these yourself.
formHelper.legalNote: This form involves your immigration status. Please work with a licensed attorney or DOJ-accredited representative.
formHelper.secureLine: Your documents are processed securely and never shared with immigration enforcement.
formHelper.contextNote: Wayfinder uses the information you have already given to answer for your situation.

findHelp.title: Find Help Near You
findHelp.subtitle: Connect with people who can help — free resettlement services, legal aid, and your state refugee office.
findHelp.attorneyWarning: Important: For any legal matter involving your immigration status, only a licensed attorney or DOJ-accredited representative can give you legal advice and represent you.
findHelp.noState: Select your state to see local organizations.

progress.title: My Progress
progress.subtitle: Track where you are with each benefit.

footer.privacy: Your information is encrypted and private. We never share it with immigration enforcement.
footer.disclaimer: Wayfinder does not provide legal advice.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Change your language or update the answers you gave during sign-up.
settings.languageHint: Choose the language for the whole app. It updates right away.
settings.editInfoHint: Change the answers you gave during sign-up. Your updated information will be used for filling out forms.

formFill.title: Fill out your form
formFill.intro: We filled in what we already know. Please review every field before you download.
formFill.secureLine: Your form is filled in your browser and never shared with immigration enforcement.
formFill.sensitive: Sensitive — do not share this with us. Enter it yourself.
formFill.noFields: This form has no fillable boxes. You can type over the highlighted spots.
formFill.loadError: We couldn't open this form. Please try another file.
formFill.uploadHint: Upload your I-94, work permit, or other document in Documents to fill more of this form automatically.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.signUp: Could not create account. Please try again.
errors.eligibility: Could not load your benefits. Please try again.
errors.upload: Upload failed. Please try a smaller file or different format.
